summary: Open PR matching four functions in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c`: `hsd_80393D2C`, `hsd_803966A0`, `hsd_80396C78`, and `fn_80397374`. The fix is mostly codegen shaping: local state pointers for global particle structs, a localized `dont_inline` pragma for call shape, and a shared `ps_remove_node` inline scheduling fold. The decomp.dev GALE01 report shows +1456 matched bytes, +0.04% matched code, four new 100.00% matches, and four nearby unmatched-function improvements.
key_files: src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c: +25/-26 across 7 hunks; only changed file; matched particle functions by adjusting `hsd_80393D2C`, shared `ps_remove_node`, `hsd_803966A0`, `hsd_80396C78`, and `fn_80397374`.
what_changed: Wrapped `hsd_80393D2C` in `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`, and replaced direct `hsd_804CF7E8.x0_b1` accesses with a local `struct ParticleConsoleState* sp = &hsd_804CF7E8` while preserving `HSD_SetReportCallback(fn_80393C14)` and `HSD_SetReportCallback(NULL)` behavior.
Changed `ps_remove_node` so `head_ptr = &sp->xD0` is folded into the null check as `if (*(head_ptr = &sp->xD0) == NULL)`, replacing the prior separate assignment plus load.

Introduced local `struct ParticleScreenState* sp = &hsd_804CF810` in `hsd_803966A0`; repeated `(&hsd_804CF810)->xBC` writes became `sp->xBC`, and `ps_remove_node(&hsd_804CF810, data)` became `ps_remove_node(sp, data)`.

In `fn_80397374`, initialized `struct ParticleScreenState* sp = &hsd_804CF810` at declaration and used `sp->xBC`, `sp->x1C`, and `ps_remove_node(sp, data)` instead of repeated `(&hsd_804CF810)->...` global-address expressions.
Removed TODO comments that described remaining partial-match issues in `hsd_803966A0`, `hsd_80396C78`, and `fn_80397374`; the PR body and bot report identify these as now matching.

GALE01 report recorded four new matches: `hsd_80396C78` 96.49% to 100.00%, `hsd_80393D2C` 94.34% to 100.00%, `hsd_803966A0` 99.87% to 100.00%, and `fn_80397374` 99.93% to 100.00%. It also reported improvements in `hsd_80395D88`, `hsd_803962A8`, `fn_80397814`, and `hsd_80396A20`.
smart_moves: Kept the `ps_remove_node` inline fold shared, as the PR body notes, so one helper scheduling adjustment fixed matched functions and also improved nearby partial functions.

Used typed local `sp` pointers to global state structs instead of repeated `(&global)->field` expressions, a small source rewrite that can steer register allocation without changing apparent behavior.
Applied `dont_inline` only around `hsd_80393D2C` with pragma push/pop, limiting the effect to the needed function and preserving intended caller call shape.
Validated with both project build commands from the PR body, `python configure.py --require-protos` and `ninja`, plus decomp.dev's GALE01 match report.
decomp_lessons: For this translation unit, use a localized `#pragma dont_inline on` guarded by push/pop when a small function must remain out-of-line for existing caller shape.
When an inline helper mismatch is about scheduling order, moving an assignment into a condition can alter emitted `addi`/`lwz` ordering while keeping C semantics simple.
For global struct state like `hsd_804CF810` or `hsd_804CF7E8`, binding the address once to a typed local pointer can improve register allocation and reduce repeated global-address codegen.
Fixing a shared inline helper can produce report improvements beyond the named target functions; track nearby unmatched functions after helper changes.
No human review rationale is available in this slice, so the reusable lessons are inferred from the PR body, diff, and bot match report rather than reviewer comments.
naming_conventions: Unmapped particle functions retain address-style names such as `hsd_80393D2C`, `hsd_803966A0`, `hsd_80396C78`, and `fn_80397374`.
Local state pointer name `sp` is used for both `struct ParticleConsoleState*` and `struct ParticleScreenState*` in this file.
`ps_remove_node` is a lower_snake_case static inline helper, contrasting with HSD-style exported names like `HSD_SetReportCallback`.
Offset-based struct fields remain named with `x` prefixes such as `x0_b1`, `xBC`, `xD0`, and `x1C`; external data labels remain address-style such as `lbl_8040BEC4`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use local `sp` aliases for global particle state to influence register choice and field access shape.
Use `#pragma dont_inline` with push/pop for a single function when inlining would disturb callsite matching.
Fold `head_ptr = &sp->xD0` into the `ps_remove_node` null check to match inline scheduling.
Prefer one shared inline-helper adjustment over callsite-specific hacks when several functions inline the same helper.
Remove stale partial-match TODOs only after report evidence shows the functions at 100.00%.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews are present in the slice.
decomp-dev bot GALE01 report: matched code became 70.11% (+0.04%, +1456 bytes) with four new matches and four improvements in unmatched particle items.
